title: ficus populnea f. citrifolia
intro: Ficus populnea f. citrifolia, also known as the Portia tree or Strangler fig, is a species of fig tree native to tropical regions of Asia and the Pacific. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Ficus populnea f. citrifolia include treating fever, skin infections, and wounds, as well as being used as a natural remedy for digestive issues and respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of Ficus populnea f. citrifolia include infusions, decoctions, and poultices, which can be made by boiling the leaves, bark, or roots in water to create a medicinal tea or paste.
